mentions.note: Revenue down 3%, Nike Direct down 7%, Digital down 9%, Converse down 35%. Gross margin fell 130 bp to 40.2%. Lost pricing power, stale products, competition rife. CEO Elliott Hill trying turnaround but taking longer than hoped. New CEO gets a chance but burden of proof is on him. Trades 24x earnings, higher than peers due to better relative position but still risky.
Cramer notes difficulty in identifying Nike turnaround; caution warranted until management clarity improves.
Wrong call on stock; CEO Elliott Hill reassembling old band when market needs innovation; Piper downgrade on lack of newness justified; give until October for turnaround.
Down 32% with attractive dividend but turnaround under Elliott Hill too slow; Cramer's mistake for travel trust; no signs of recovery yet.
Cramer notes Nike and Home Depot are his two worst stocks; both represent major headwinds for consumer and retail brands.

Owns in charitable trust and nervous; no line of sight to return to greatness yet; China intractable, fevered competition, old inventory still dogging; needs new innovation and US green shoots to restore confidence.
Mixed execution recently; Cramer patient but impatient, believes in turnaround. Not selling; waiting for $70 target. Huge turn underway.

Product quality issue not mentioned in CEO Elliot Hill earnings call; turnaround takes 1–2 years minimum, not 2–5 quarters; charitable trust owns it with patience; disappointing but betting on long-term recovery.
Turnaround play rebounding on insider buying by Tim Cook (Apple CEO), ex-Intel board member, and new CEO; signals business genuinely turning.
